name: GHRKO mice shared insulin/IGF signaling features with caloric restriction
summary: The GHRKO and caloric-restriction study supports mechanistic overlap between GH/IGF attenuation, low insulin, and nutrient-sensing longevity biology.
statement: Long-lived growth hormone receptor knockout mice showed very low insulin, undetectable IGF-I, reduced Akt signaling, and Foxo/PGC-1alpha-linked changes that overlap caloric-restriction nutrient-sensing biology.
population_or_model: Growth hormone receptor knockout mice and caloric-restricted controls
time_horizon: Cross-sectional signaling analysis in long-lived mouse models
quantitative_note: The source reports GHRKO mice had very low circulating insulin and undetectable IGF-I.
caveats: This is mechanistic mouse evidence rather than a direct aging intervention test in humans.
Germline GHRKO dwarf mice are not a practical intervention model.
Overlap with caloric restriction does not prove the same translation path or safety profile.
